info.title: DX Data Cloud API
info.description:
The Data Cloud API is an interface for pushing data into your DX data lake for reporting. Use it for deployment data, incidents, custom data and metrics, and more.

This is a collection of HTTP RPC-style methods with URLs in the form `https://yourinstance.getdx.net/api/METHOD_FAMILY.method`.

Requests can be sent as JSON or URL-encoded form data. Set `Content-Type: application/json` when sending JSON.

Successful requests return `{"ok": true}`. Errors return `{"ok": false, "error": "error_code"}`.

tags.name: aiToolMetrics
tags.description: Add daily usage data for bespoke AI tools. Records are stored in the `bespoke_ai_tool_daily_metrics` table queryable in Data Studio.

tags.name: customData
tags.description: Add arbitrary JSON data to your DX database. Records are stored in the `custom_data` table queryable alongside all other DX data.

tags.name: deployments
tags.description: Create deployments in DX from any data source. API-created deployments offer a flexible approach that can handle a wide range of deployment mechanisms.

tags.name: incidents
tags.description: Create or update incidents in DX.

tags.name: pipelineRuns
tags.description: Capture CI pipeline data in DX where a native data connector does not exist.

tags.name: repoGroups
tags.description: Organize repositories into hierarchical groups for filtering in the dashboard. Note: repoGroups API endpoints need to be manually enabled on your DX account.

post.description: Add daily usage data for bespoke AI tools by email, date, and tool combinations.

id.description: Generated unique ID for the record.

post.description: Add multiple daily usage data records for bespoke AI tools by email, date, and tool combinations in a single request.

data.description: Array of AI tool metrics records.

200.description: Success - returns array of created records

post.description: Delete a record based on unique reference and key pair, or ID.

id.description: Generated unique ID for the record.

post.summary: Delete all custom data by reference
post.description: Delete all records with the given reference.

get.description: Retrieve a custom data record based on unique reference and key pair, or ID.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Generated unique ID for the record. Use instead of reference+key.

get.summary: Get all custom data by reference
get.description: Get all custom data records with the given reference.

post.description: Create or update custom data by key or reference.

timestamp.description: ISO-8601 timestamp. Defaults to time of request if not specified.

post.description: Create or update multiple custom data records, each containing a reference and key pair along with a JSON object value. If any items are malformed, the entire request will be rejected.

data.description: A list of custom data objects. See customData.set for structure.

post.description: Create a new deployment. DX supports attributing deployments to code changes via commit SHA, merge commit SHAs, or timestamps.

repository.description: Repository name including org prefix. For Bitbucket Cloud: repo name only. For Bitbucket DC: project_key/repo_name. For ADO: project_name/repo_name. For Gerrit: project name only.

commit_sha.description: Commit SHA deployed to production (7-40 chars). If not provided, attribution uses deployed_at timestamps.

merge_commit_shas.description: Array of commit SHAs being deployed, for attributing deploys to pull requests. Each 7-40 chars.

reference_id.description: Unique identifier for the deployment. If not provided, a random UUID is generated. DX returns 409 if a subsequent request for the same reference_id changes values.

source_url.description: External URL with more deployment info. Displayed in the DX UI.

source_name.description: Source for the deployment to differentiate deploy systems.

integration_branch.description: Set if using a branch to integrate changes before merging to a release branch.

409.description: Conflict - reference_id already exists with different values

post.description: Associate a monorepo pull request with specific services. Use along with deployments.create for monorepos to ensure PRs are attributed to correct services when deployment data is captured.

services.description: Array of services changed in this merge.

repository.description: GitHub repository name including org prefix.

services.description: Array of services changed in this merge.

post.description: Create or update an incident using reference_id for idempotent updates.

reference_id.description: Unique identifier for the incident to support idempotent updates.

started_at.description: ISO-8601 timestamp for when the incident started.

services.description: Array of services related to this incident.

source_url.description: URL to the incident in your incident management system.

resolved_at.description: ISO-8601 timestamp for when the incident was resolved.

metadata.description: Additional data about the incident. Keys may include letters, numbers, dashes and underscores.

post.summary: Create or update a pipeline run
post.description: Insert or update a pipeline workflow run. Multiple requests with the same reference_id are collected into one pipeline workflow run record. Status priority: failure > cancelled > success > running > unknown.

pipeline_name.description: Name of the pipeline that this job is for.

pipeline_source.description: Which project source (e.g. CircleCI, Jenkins, Tekton).

reference_id.description: A globally unique identifier for this run.

started_at.description: ISO-8601 timestamp. Requests don't need same started_at; earliest value is used.

finished_at.description: ISO-8601 timestamp for when the pipeline run finished.

repository.description: The full name of the repository.

commit_sha.description: Commit SHA the build ran on. If provided, repository is required.

pr_number.description: Pull request number the build ran on. If provided, repository is required.

source_url.description: Web address to view pipeline run details.

head_branch.description: Branch the pipeline run was run against.

email.description: Email of the user who initiated the build.

github_username.description: GitHub username of the user who initiated the build.

gitlab_username.description: GitLab username of the user who initiated the build.

post.summary: Add repos to a repo group
post.description: Add repositories to an existing repo group without affecting existing repos. Repositories can only belong to one repo group at a time.

id.description: The ID of the repo group. Either id or reference_id must be provided.

reference_id.description: The unique reference ID for the repo group. Either id or reference_id must be provided.

repos.description: Array of repository objects to add. external_id is the repo id assigned by the source (e.g. GitHub). Retrievable via repos table in Data Studio.

post.description: Delete a repo group by ID or reference_id. Removes the group and unassociates all repositories (repos themselves are not deleted).

id.description: Generated unique ID for the repo group. Either id or reference_id must be provided.

reference_id.description: Unique reference ID for the repo group. Either id or reference_id must be provided.

get.description: Retrieve a repo group by ID or reference_id.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Generated unique ID for the repo group.

parameters.name: reference_id
parameters.description: Unique reference ID for the repo group.

post.summary: Remove repos from a repo group
post.description: Remove repositories from an existing repo group. Repos themselves are not deleted, only the association. All specified repos must be in the target group.

id.description: The ID of the repo group. Either id or reference_id must be provided.

reference_id.description: Unique reference ID for the repo group. Either id or reference_id must be provided.

repos.description: Array of repository objects to remove from the group.

post.summary: Create or update a repo group
post.description: Create or update a repo group. When updating, providing repos replaces all existing repo associations.

name.description: The name of the repo group.

id.description: ID of existing repo group to update.

parent_id.description: ID of the parent repo group for hierarchy.

repos.description: Array of repository objects to associate. external_id is the repo id assigned by the source. Retrievable via repos table in Data Studio.

bearerAuth.description: API token generated in the DX admin area. Pass as Authorization: Bearer <token>

ServiceObject.description: Service reference object. The name field is optional.

name.description: Optional display name for the service.

RepoReference.description: Repository reference. source and external_id can be retrieved from the repos table in Data Studio.

external_id.description: Repo ID assigned by the source provider.
